title: Product vs Period Costs
event: Reyes Manufacturing incurs: direct materials $30,000, direct labor $25,000, factory overhead $20,000, selling expenses $12,000, and administrative expenses $8,000. Compute total product cost.

computationPaths.narration: Product costs attach to inventory and wait for the sale; period costs expense immediately. The factory wall is the dividing line.

memorize.body: Inside the factory = product cost (DM + DL + OH). Outside the factory = period cost (selling + admin).
Prime cost = DM + DL. Conversion cost = DL + OH. DL sits in both — the overlap is a favorite quiz question.

questions.prompt: DM $30,000 · DL $25,000 · OH $20,000 · selling $12,000 · admin $8,000. Total product cost = ?

distractors.feedback: You capitalized the period costs. Selling and admin expense immediately — they never enter inventory.
That's PRIME cost (DM + DL). Product cost also carries factory overhead: 75,000.
